prompt:
Take the provided reference image (a line-art illustration of an upright protector tube with a black cap on top and a white body below) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in every element — same tube shape and proportions, same cap, same body, same line-art style on white background, same line weight, same outlined embossed letter style.

The HEALTH text on the WHITE BODY is CORRECT in the reference — keep it exactly as-is (first H at the TOP of the body, last H at the bottom, letters running top-down along the body's length axis in book-spine sideways orientation). DO NOT touch the body or its HEALTH text.

>>> THE ONLY MODIFICATION <<< — the DIALED text on the BLACK CAP is currently rendered REVERSED. In the reference, the first D of DIALED is at the BOTTOM of the cap (at the cap/body seam) and the last D is at the TOP of the cap. This is wrong.

FLIP the DIALED text 180° on the cap so the letter sequence is REVERSED end-to-end:

  • The letter that was at the BOTTOM of the cap (the first D of DIALED) moves to the TOP of the cap.
  • The letter that was at the TOP of the cap (the last D of DIALED) moves to the BOTTOM of the cap.
  • The middle letters (I, A, L, E) also reverse order accordingly.
  • After the flip, reading top-down on the cap: D (topmost) → I → A → L → E → D (bottommost) = 'DIALED' spelled forward.

The letter style stays IDENTICAL — same book-spine sideways orientation, same thin white outline strokes against the solid-black cap fill, same bold sans-serif capitals, same line-art embossed look. Only the letter SEQUENCE on the cap is reversed end-to-end.

After this fix, the assembled tube reads top-down as: BLACK CAP showing DIALED forward (D-I-A-L-E-D) → WHITE BODY showing HEALTH forward (H-E-A-L-T-H) = 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly top-down.

Do NOT modify anything else — same tube proportions, same composition, same body, same correct HEALTH text, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic. The ONLY change is the 180° flip of the DIALED text on the black cap so the first D is now at the top.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers.
